summary.note: The single finding (backup JSON file) is pre-existing (introduced_in_diff: false) and advisory/non-blocking per AC-12.1 default-safe rule. Both changed files are clean.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Skill(codex) executed the /codex command instead of acting as a consultant — the skill misinterpreted the args as $ARGUMENTS and ran in exec mode, producing a reprintof the codex.md command body rather than feedback. Output was unparseable as CODEX_FEEDBACK.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted — 5 adversarial questions addressed: (1) False positives: none found; backup file is correctly identified as pre-existing. (2) Severity calibration: corrected duplicate_scripts/major to duplicate_content/minor — backup has different MD5 from original (not byte-identical), so 'major' (identical duplicates) was wrong. (3) Safety Rules compliance: .json.bak is not documentation, safe_to_delete applies with no functional references; Safety Rules 'Archive Rather Than Delete' applies only to documentation files. (4) Scope accuracy: both changed files have zero cleanliness violations in this diff; assessment is correct. (5) Wording precision: reclassified finding from duplicate_scripts to duplicate_content (rule 17) which matches a non-script JSON backup.
